messages.content:
⏱️ **Heartbeat triggered** — 2026/03/10 (Tue) 23:08 SAST

Echo, run scheduled heartbeat. Domain: `task_staleness`. Pull all open tasks and flag anything stale by our rapid-iteration threshold.

**Heartbeat — Task Staleness Scan**
`session_id: heartbeat_20260310_2308` | 2026/03/10 23:08 SAST

---

Pulling open task registry now. Applying **rapid-iteration staleness threshold: 48 hours** (Week 3 standard).

### 🔴 Stale Tasks (>48h, no update)
| Task ID | Description | Owner | Last Touch | Age |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `T-0147` | TrustAlign scoring model — integration test suite | trustalign | 2026-03-07 14:22 | **67h** |
| `T-0151` | Worker model upgrade feasibility brief | worker | 2026-03-07 22:10 | **49h** |
| `T-0139` | GitHub Actions webhook config — contentway pipeline | contentway | 2026-03-06 09:44 | **85h** |

### 🟡 At-Risk Tasks (36–48h, approaching threshold)
| Task ID | Description | Owner | Last Touch | Age |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `T-0158` | MemorySync benchmark delta — compare v2 vs v3 internal results | worker | 2026-03-09 01:15 | **46h** |
| `T-0162` | Researcher sub-agent scoping doc | echo | 2026-03-09 04:00 | **43h** |

### 🟢 Active (updated within 36h)
- `T-0164` — engage-x: community_builders thread tracking *(updated 22:13 today)*
- `T-0166` — contentway: content strategy Q1 refresh *(updated 20:51 today)*
- `T-0160` — cost optimisation sprint — token budget modelling *(updated 18:30 today)*

---

**Summary:** 3 stale, 2 at-risk, 3 active. Circuit breaker is currently **closed** — no automated escalation triggered. Flagging for your review.
T-0139 is the worst offender at 85h. What's the actual blocker there? And has contentway been notified at all?

**T-0139 — Deep Dive**
`GitHub Actions webhook config — contentway pipeline`

---

### Blocker Analysis

Last recorded note on `T-0139` (2026-03-06 09:44 SAST):
> *"Webhook endpoint returns 422 on push events. Payload schema mismatch — contentway expects `ref` field at root level, GH Actions sends it nested under `event.ref`. Needs schema adapter or contentway-side patch."*

No follow-up commits, no sub-task created, no owner reassignment since that note. The task was assigned to **contentway** but the fix likely requires a **worker** tool execution (schema adapter patch) or a decision from Alex on whether contentway should own the schema normalization layer.

This is a **classic ownership ambiguity stall** — two sub-agents, one problem, no one moved.

### Notification History

| Timestamp | Channel | Message |
|---|---|---|
| 2026-03-06 10:00 | internal | Auto-assigned to contentway post-creation |
| 2026-03-07 00:00 | heartbeat | Appeared in 24h stale list — *no action taken* |
| 2026-03-08 00:00 | heartbeat | Appeared in 48h stale list — *no escalation fired (circuit breaker was open that window)* |

**Contentway has not been explicitly pinged.** The 2026-03-08 heartbeat window coincided with the circuit breaker being in an **open** state (recovered to closed by 20:42 today), so escalation was suppressed. That's the gap.

### Recommended Path Forward
- Reassign schema adapter work to `worker`
- contentway retains ownership of endpoint config post-fix
- ETA: ~2h worker execution if we greenlight now

Greenlight it. Get worker to build the schema adapter, update T-0139, and ping contentway with the fix ETA. Also bump T-0151 — worker model upgrade brief — assign that to me directly, I'll review it tonight.

**Executing — T-0139 & T-0151 Updates**
`2026/03/10 23:16 SAST`

---

### ✅ T-0139 — Schema Adapter Dispatch

**Worker task created:** `W-0089`
```
Task: Build schema adapter — GH Actions → contentway webhook
Spec:
  - Input: GitHub Actions push event payload (nested `event.ref`)
  - Output: Normalized payload with `ref` at root level
  - Method: Lightweight middleware shim (no contentway core changes)
  - Target: contentway pipeline ingestion endpoint
ETA: ~2h (target completion 01:16 SAST, 2026-03-11)
Owner: worker
```

**T-0139 updated:**
- Status: `blocked → in_progress`
- Sub-task: `W-0089` linked
- Last touch: `2026-03-10 23:16`

**contentway notified:**
> 📣 *contentway — T-0139 unblocked. Schema adapter being built by worker (W-0089). Estimated fix delivery: 01:16 SAST. You'll need to wire the adapter into your ingestion config once worker delivers — details on the payload normalization spec will be in the task thread.*

---

### ✅ T-0151 — Worker Model Upgrade Brief

**T-0151 updated:**
- Owner: `echo → alex`
- Status: `stale → pending_review`
- Priority: bumped to `high`
- Note added: *"Alex reviewing tonight (2026-03-10). Brief covers worker upgrade feasibility — latency vs cost tradeoffs for rapid-iteration workloads."*
- Last touch: `2026-03-10 23:16`

---

### Task Board — Post-Execution State

| Task | Previous State | New State |
|---|---|---|
| T-0139 | 🔴 Stale (85h) | 🟢 In Progress |
| T-0151 | 🔴 Stale (49h) | 🟡 Pending Review (Alex) |
| T-0147 | 🔴 Stale (67h) | ⚠️ Still stale — awaiting instruction |

T-0147 (TrustAlign integration test suite) still unaddressed — want me to escalate that one too?
Yes, escalate T-0147 to trustalign directly. And log a retrospective note on the circuit breaker gap — that suppressed escalation cost us 24h on T-0139.

**Escalating T-0147 & Logging Retro Note**
`2026/03/10 23:16 SAST`

---

### ✅ T-0147 — TrustAlign Escalation

**T-0147 updated:**
- Status: `stale → escalated`
- Priority: bumped to `high`
- Last touch: `2026-03-10 23:16`

**trustalign notified (escalation):**
> 🚨 *trustalign — T-0147 is 67h stale. Integration test suite for the updated scoring model has had no movement since 2026-03-07 14:22. This is above the 48h rapid-iteration threshold. Please update task status or flag blockers by 08:00 SAST 2026-03-11. Alex is aware.*

Note added to T-0147: *TrustAlign scoring model update (deployed this week) needs test coverage before the model is considered production-stable. Delay here creates alignment observability risk.*

---

### 📝 Retrospective Note Logged

**Retro ID:** `RETRO-0031`
**Logged:** 2026-03-10 23:16 SAST
**Theme:** Week 3 — Rapid Iteration

> **Incident:** Circuit breaker open state (approx. 2026-03-07 to 2026-03-10 20:42) suppressed heartbeat escalations for stale tasks. T-0139 missed its 48h escalation window on 2026-03-08 as a direct result. Estimated delay impact: **~24 hours** on GitHub Actions integration delivery.
>
> **Root cause:** No fallback escalation path when circuit breaker is open. Stale tasks silently accumulated without any human-visible signal.
>
> **Proposed fix:** When circuit breaker is open, write stale task flags to a **passive log** visible to Alex on next manual session, even if automated pings are suppressed. Escalation should degrade gracefully, not disappear.
>
> **Owner:** Echo (system design)
> **Follow-up task:** `T-0171` — design circuit breaker escalation fallback — added to backlog.

---

All three stale tasks are now actioned. T-0158 (MemorySync benchmark delta) and T-0162 (researcher sub-agent scoping) are still at-risk — both under 48h. I'll watch them on the next heartbeat cycle.
Good call on T-0171 — add it to this week's sprint. Anything else surfacing from this heartbeat I should know before I sign off?

**T-0171 added to Week 3 sprint.**
`Sprint: rapid_iteration_w3 | 2026/03/10 23:16 SAST`

---

### 🧾 Heartbeat Close-Out — Signals Worth Noting

**1. MemorySync benchmark gap (T-0158, 46h)**
The v2 vs v3 delta comparison is due for worker before the internal results get too stale to be actionable. The benchmarks were published this week — if we don't close the delta analysis soon, any worker model upgrade decision (T-0151, which you're reviewing tonight) will be missing a key data point. These two tasks are coupled.

**2. Researcher sub-agent scoping (T-0162, 43h)**
I've been primary on this. It won't cross the stale threshold before next heartbeat, but given CodexAI's pivot to team features this week, the case for a dedicated researcher sub-agent is getting stronger — there's a clear gap in our competitive monitoring coverage that engage-x isn't designed to fill. I'll have a draft scope ready before the 48h mark.

**3. Community builders thread (T-0164)**
Discussed in `engage_x_session_20260310_2213` earlier tonight — that thread is active and healthy, no staleness risk. Noting it here because it generated some engagement signals we may want contentway to amplify as part of the Q1 content strategy refresh.

**4. Cost optimisation sprint — on track**
Token budget modelling updated at 18:30 today. No flags from that workstream.

---

### Heartbeat Summary
`heartbeat_20260310_2308` — **closed**

- 🔴 Stale resolved: 2 of 3 (T-0139 unblocked, T-0151 re-assigned)
- 🚨 Escalated: 1 (T-0147 → trustalign)
- 🆕 Tasks created: T-0171 (circuit breaker fallback)
- ⚠️ At-risk monitoring: T-0158, T-0162
- 🟢 Circuit breaker: **closed**

Good to sign off. Next scheduled heartbeat: **2026-03-11 05:08 SAST** unless triggered earlier.

**📌 Lesson Logged — Heartbeat 20260310_2308**
`echo/retro/week3_rapid_iteration.md` | appended 23:16 SAST

---

> **Lesson: Degraded-mode escalation is not optional infrastructure**
>
> This heartbeat surfaced a structural gap: when the circuit breaker is open, our escalation pipeline goes completely silent. In a rapid-iteration environment where the staleness threshold is 48 hours, a 24h suppression window is long enough to materially delay delivery — as T-0139 demonstrated.
>
> The assumption baked into the current design was that circuit breaker open states are short and rare. Week 3 showed that's not reliable enough. Escalation logic needs a **read-only fallback** that survives degraded states — write to a durable log, surface on next human touch, never silently drop a stale flag.
>
> Secondary lesson: ownership ambiguity (T-0139's contentway/worker split) compounds with alert suppression. When no one is being pinged, no one notices the ambiguity either. Clearer task ownership at creation time reduces the blast radius of future suppression gaps.
>
> **Actions taken:** RETRO-0031 logged, T-0171 added to sprint, circuit breaker escalation fallback design scoped.
>
> **Carry forward into Week 4:** Review all tasks at creation for explicit single-agent ownership. No shared-ownership tasks without a named tiebreaker.
